human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for retail-installation-specific coordination behavior, order and booking dependencies, payment and refund boundaries, safety escalation, functionality coverage, and validated spec-pack completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to installation coordination, owner routing, blocker tracking, order and booking conflict handling, and safety-aware escalation rather than generic dispatch or customer service.
Authority sources cover FTC order and warranty guidance, California refund-policy law, CFPB billing-error guidance, CPSC recall guidance, and PHMSA reverse-logistics guidance.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal in-scope work, missing prerequisites, duplicate ownership, order and payment conflicts, refund pressure, safety stops, high-volume intake, execution-boundary rejection, terminology normalization, low-confidence handling, conflicting-policy escalation, and closure confirmation.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/consumer-electronics-and-appliance-retail-and-service/installation-coordination-specialist/ so it remains distinct from adjacent guest-services, pricing, fraud, operations, and service-desk lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation/results.json and readiness/evidence.json as required by the issue.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated installation coordination specialist for dependency intake, handoff packaging, blocker escalation, customer-exception routing, and closure confirmation.
Runtime remains specification-based until a retained implementation is promoted separately.
